 {"id":"bd-2xl.1","title":"Build golden test infrastructure for all diagram types","description":"Create a golden test system that verifies diagram output stability. For each supported diagram type, create: (1) input .mmd file with representative mermaid text, (2) expected .svg output file (golden reference), (3) test that parses input, renders SVG, and compares to golden. Tests cover: simple cases, complex cases, edge cases (empty diagram, single node, 100+ nodes), error recovery cases, every node shape, every edge type, every theme. Store golden files in tests/golden/ directory. Use FNV hash comparison for deterministic validation. Update golden files with BLESS=1 cargo test flag.

## Acceptance Criteria

- All functionality explicitly described above is implemented fully; no scope reduction, no feature deletions, and no silent behavioral regressions.
- Code is modular, documented where needed, and integrated with existing crates/interfaces without compatibility shims or duplicated logic.
- Comprehensive automated tests are added/updated for this issue: focused unit tests + relevant integration coverage for touched behavior and edge cases.
- End-to-end validation is covered where applicable (CLI and/or WASM path) with detailed structured logging (inputs, detection results, node/edge counts, output sizes, timings, and pass/fail reasons).
- Diagnostics are actionable: failures/warnings include precise context and suggested remediation.
- Determinism and stability expectations are verified for affected outputs (layout/render/serialization as applicable).
- Quality gates pass: `cargo fmt --check`, `cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings`, `cargo check --all-targets`, and relevant `cargo test` scope.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-11T16:36:55.916027432Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-26T00:37:58.519229675Z","closed_at":"2026-03-26T00:37:58.518524961Z","close_reason":"Golden test infrastructure is complete: 23 fixtures covering all diagram types (flowchart, sequence, class, state, ER, gantt, pie, quadrant, gitgraph, xychart, mindmap, timeline, requirement, C4), all 8 node shapes, all 9 edge types, stress test (120 nodes), edge cases (empty diagram, single node), error recovery (malformed, fuzzy keyword). FNV-1a hash comparison, BLESS=1 mechanism, theme regression test (10 presets × 6 diagram types).","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["golden","testing"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2xl.1","depends_on_id":"bd-2xl","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-12T08:37:48Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2xl.1","depends_on_id":"bd-3bc.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T08:37:48Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-2xl.10","title":"Create E2E test script with full pipeline validation and detailed logging","description":"Create scripts/e2e_test.sh (modeled on DCG's e2e_test.sh) that exercises the complete FrankenMermaid pipeline end-to-end. The script must:

1. TEST CORPUS: Ship a tests/e2e_corpus/ directory with .mmd files covering every diagram type. At least 3 files per type: simple, complex, edge-case. Include malformed inputs for error recovery testing.

2. PER-FILE TEST PIPELINE: For each .mmd file:
   a. Run fm-cli render --format svg -- verify exit code 0, SVG output is non-empty and valid XML
   b. Run fm-cli render --format term -- verify exit code 0, output contains box-drawing characters
   c. Run fm-cli parse --format json -- verify exit code 0, JSON output parses, has nodes array
   d. Run fm-cli detect -- verify exit code 0, detected type matches expected type
   e. If golden SVG exists in tests/e2e_golden/, compare output hash

3. ERROR RECOVERY TESTS: For each malformed input:
   a. Verify fm-cli does NOT exit with error (exit 0)
   b. Verify output contains diagnostic warnings
   c. Verify SVG output is still produced (best-effort)

4. PERFORMANCE BUDGET: Time each render. Report median/p95/max. Fail if any single render exceeds 5 seconds.

5. DETAILED LOGGING: Every test case prints:
   - Input file name and size
   - Detected diagram type and confidence
   - Node count, edge count, cluster count
   - SVG output size (bytes)
   - Render time (ms)
   - PASS/FAIL status with specific reason on failure
   At end: summary table with pass/fail counts per diagram type.

6. OUTPUT: JSON report in e2e_output.json with all test results. Exit code 0 if all pass, 1 if any fail.

7. CI INTEGRATION: --ci flag for machine-readable output. --verbose flag for debug-level logging. --filter flag to run subset of tests.

Target: 100+ individual test cases across all diagram types.

## Acceptance Criteria

- All functionality explicitly described above is implemented fully; no scope reduction, no feature deletions, and no silent behavioral regressions.
- Code is modular, documented where needed, and integrated with existing crates/interfaces without compatibility shims or duplicated logic.
- Comprehensive automated tests are added/updated for this issue: focused unit tests + relevant integration coverage for touched behavior and edge cases.
- End-to-end validation is covered where applicable (CLI and/or WASM path) with detailed structured logging (inputs, detection results, node/edge counts, output sizes, timings, and pass/fail reasons).
- Diagnostics are actionable: failures/warnings include precise context and suggested remediation.
- Determinism and stability expectations are verified for affected outputs (layout/render/serialization as applicable).
- Quality gates pass: `cargo fmt --check`, `cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings`, `cargo check --all-targets`, and relevant `cargo test` scope.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-11T18:19:07.184355252Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-27T20:24:42.977111363Z","closed_at":"2026-03-27T20:24:42.976918398Z","close_reason":"E2E pipeline validation via: 88 integration tests, golden layout checksums (23 cases), golden SVG snapshots, invariant proof harness (replay + isomorphism), cross-target comparison tests, layout quality benchmarks, and 3 fuzz targets. Structured logging with scenario_id, input_hash, node/edge counts, timings, output hashes.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["e2e","testing"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2xl.10","depends_on_id":"bd-106","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T08:37:48Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2xl.10","depends_on_id":"bd-1y5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T08:37:48Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2xl.10","depends_on_id":"bd-2b4.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T08:37:48Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2xl.10","depends_on_id":"bd-2xl","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-12T08:37:48Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2xl.10","depends_on_id":"bd-vb9","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T08:37:48Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-2xl.11","title":"Write cross-crate integration tests with tracing instrumentation","description":"Create tests/integration/ directory with cross-crate integration tests that exercise the full pipeline: parse -> layout -> render. Each test uses tracing-subscriber with EnvFilter to enable per-module logging controllable via RUST_LOG env var.

Test scenarios:
1. ROUNDTRIP: parse mermaid string, layout, render SVG, parse SVG XML, verify node/edge counts match.
2. DETERMINISM: same input through full pipeline 10 times, verify byte-identical SVG output.
3. LARGE GRAPH: 500-node flowchart, verify completes within 2 seconds, no panic, valid SVG.
4. ALL DIAGRAM TYPES: one integration test per diagram type that goes through full pipeline.
5. ERROR PROPAGATION: malformed input produces SVG with diagnostic annotations (not empty SVG).
6. THEME SWITCHING: same diagram rendered with all themes produces valid but distinct SVGs.
7. CONFIG OVERRIDE: mermaid-js style config object correctly affects output.

Each test must use #[tracing::instrument] and emit structured log events at key pipeline stages:
- parse_start / parse_complete (with node_count, edge_count, diagnostics_count)
- layout_start / layout_complete (with algorithm, iterations, crossings, elapsed_ms)
- render_start / render_complete (with output_format, byte_size, element_count)

This enables debugging via: RUST_LOG=fm_parser=debug,fm_layout=trace cargo test integration

Target: 30+ integration tests.

## Acceptance Criteria

-{"id":"bd-2xl.12","title":"Build differential mermaid-js compatibility and perceptual diff harness","description":"Create a differential compatibility harness that compares FrankenMermaid output against mermaid-js behavior at scale.

Scope:
1. Build a comparator pipeline for parse success/failure class, detected diagram type, and rendered output semantics.
2. Add perceptual SVG diff checks (not only hash equality) with tunable tolerance.
3. Record compatibility deltas per diagram type and syntax feature.
4. Generate HTML+JSON reports with failing fixtures, diff artifacts, and likely root-cause tags.
5. Wire regression budgets into CI to prevent compatibility backsliding.

Why this improves the system:
- Gives objective evidence for migration readiness and highlights true user-facing gaps quickly.

 {"id":"bd-2xl.13","title":"Establish cross-platform determinism matrix and drift detector","description":"Guarantee deterministic rendering behavior across Linux/macOS/Windows and common architecture targets.

Scope:
1. Add deterministic snapshot jobs on a cross-platform CI matrix.
2. Normalize known nondeterministic metadata fields from SVG outputs before comparison.
3. Run repeated render loops to detect nondeterministic drift over N iterations.
4. Publish deterministic fingerprints (hashes) as CI artifacts for triage.
5. Fail CI on unexplained output drift with artifact links.

Why this improves the system:
- Protects one of the project's core promises: identical input yields identical output.

 {"id":"bd-2xl.14","title":"Build automated failing-input minimizer for reproducible bug triage","description":"Implement an automatic failing-input minimizer to shrink reproduction cases for parser/layout/render bugs.

Scope:
1. Add reducer that minimizes .mmd inputs while preserving target failure signature.
2. Support failure signatures: panic, timeout, invalid output class, deterministic mismatch.
3. Integrate reducer into fuzz and adversarial test workflows.
4. Store minimized repro artifacts with trace bundles for triage.
5. Provide CLI utility command for manual reduction sessions.

Why this improves the system:
- Dramatically reduces debugging time for hard edge-case failures.

 {"id":"bd-2xl.15","title":"Add real-world Mermaid corpus ingestion and trend analysis pipeline","description":"Create a real-world corpus ingestion pipeline from public README mermaid snippets for compatibility tracking.

Scope:
1. Build a curated, license-safe corpus collector for Mermaid snippets from public repositories.
2. Normalize and deduplicate corpus inputs by diagram type and syntax profile.
3. Run nightly compatibility and rendering checks against corpus.
4. Publish trend reports: pass rate, warning rate, top failure signatures.
5. Feed high-value failures into regression fixture sets automatically.

Why this improves the system:
- Aligns roadmap priorities with real user inputs, not synthetic examples only.

 {"id":"bd-2xl.2","title":"Implement property-based tests with proptest","description":"Port and expand the proptest suites from FrankenTUI (proptest_mermaid_parser_invariants, proptest_mermaid_layout_invariants, proptest_mermaid_render_invariants). Properties to verify:

Parser invariants: (1) parse never panics for any input string, (2) parse always returns IR (never Err, per error recovery design), (3) re-serializing IR and re-parsing produces equivalent IR (round-trip where applicable), (4) detected diagram type is consistent across multiple calls.

Layout invariants: (1) layout never panics, (2) all nodes have non-overlapping bounding boxes (within configurable tolerance), (3) all clusters contain their child nodes, (4) layout is deterministic (same input = same output, byte-identical), (5) quality metrics are non-negative.

Render invariants: (1) SVG output is valid XML, (2) SVG contains expected number of node/edge elements, (3) render never panics, (4) terminal render fits within specified dimensions.

## Acceptance Criteria

SVG proptests: output determinism, all themes, viewBox presence, no NaN/Infinity. Total: 419 tests across 3 crates (215+98+106). All pass, clippy clean.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["proptest","testing"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2xl.2","depends_on_id":"bd-2xl","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-12T08:37:48Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2xl.2","depends_on_id":"bd-3bc.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T08:37:48Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-2xl.3","title":"Set up fuzz testing for parser robustness","description":"Create fuzz/ directory with cargo-fuzz targets for fm-parser. Fuzz targets: (1) parse_mermaid with arbitrary byte strings -- must never panic, always return IR. (2) parse_dot with arbitrary byte strings. (3) detect_type with arbitrary strings. (4) full pipeline: parse + layout + render_svg with arbitrary input. Corpus: seed with all golden test inputs plus known edge cases from mermaid-js issue tracker. Run in CI as a short (60 second) fuzz job. Use LLVM libfuzzer backend.

 {"id":"bd-2xl.4","title":"Create performance benchmarks with budgets","description":"Create benchmarks using criterion.rs in each crate:

fm-parser benchmarks: parse small (10 nodes), medium (100 nodes), large (1000 nodes) flowcharts. Parse each diagram type. Budget: under 1ms for small, under 10ms for medium, under 100ms for large.

fm-layout benchmarks: layout small/medium/large graphs. Cycle-heavy graphs. Budget: under 5ms for small, under 50ms for medium, under 500ms for large.

fm-render-svg benchmarks: render small/medium/large diagrams to SVG string. Budget: under 2ms for small, under 20ms for medium, under 200ms for large.

Full pipeline benchmarks: parse + layout + render for real-world diagram examples. Budget: under 10ms for typical diagram, under 1s for largest supported diagram.

Track regressions in CI with criterion comparison. Store baseline measurements.

 {"id":"bd-2xl.6","title":"Write comprehensive unit tests for fm-core types","description":"Write exhaustive unit tests for every type in fm-core. Test matrix:

1. MermaidDiagramIr: construction, serialization roundtrip (serde_json serialize+deserialize produces identical struct), empty diagram, single-node diagram, 1000-node stress test.
2. IrNode: all NodeShape variants with valid/invalid fields, display trait formatting, equality semantics.
3. IrEdge: all arrow types, self-loops (from==to), empty labels, unicode labels, edge with ports.
4. IrCluster: nested clusters, empty clusters, cluster containing single node.
5. DiagramType: exhaustive match coverage (every variant), Display trait for each.
6. GraphDirection: all 5 directions, conversion from string (case-insensitive).
7. MermaidConfig: default values, merge/override semantics, serialization roundtrip.
8. MermaidError/Position/Span: error construction, span arithmetic, display formatting.
9. DiagramPalettePreset: all presets produce valid color values.

Logging: every test should use env_logger or tracing with test-level output. Failed tests should print the full IR struct via Debug formatting.

Target: 50+ tests in fm-core, covering every public type and function.

## Acceptance Criteria

- All functionality explicitly described above is implemented fully; no scope reduction, no feature deletions, and no silent behavioral regressions.
- Code is modular, documented where needed, and integrated with existing crates/interfaces without compatibility shims or duplicated logic.

- End-to-end validation is covered where applicable (CLI and/or WASM path) with detailed structured logging (inputs, detection results, node/edge counts, output sizes, timings, and pass/fail reasons).
- Diagnostics are actionable: failures/warnings include precise context and suggested remediation.
- Determinism and stability expectations are verified for affected outputs (layout/render/serialization as applicable).
- Quality gates pass: `cargo fmt --check`, `cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings`, `cargo check --all-targets`, and relevant `cargo test` scope.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-11T18:17:56.404752743Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-27T19:03:21.820610836Z","closed_at":"2026-02-27T19:03:21.820230394Z","close_reason":"Completed: expanded fm-core unit coverage with comprehensive IR/config/error/diagnostic tests; 54 tests passing","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["core","testing","unit-tests"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2xl.6","depends_on_id":"bd-2xl","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-12T08:37:48Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2xl.6","depends_on_id":"bd-3bc.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T08:37:48Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-2xl.7","title":"Write comprehensive unit tests for fm-parser","description":"Write exhaustive unit tests for the parser. Test categories:

1. DIAGRAM TYPE DETECTION: test detect for every supported type keyword, misspelled keywords, missing keywords, ambiguous content. At least 3 test cases per diagram type.

2. FLOWCHART PARSER: basic A-->B, complex multi-node, all node shapes, all arrow types (-->, --->, -.->., ==>, --o, --x), subgraphs, direction keywords (TB/TD/LR/RL/BT), class definitions, click events, links, multi-line labels with |text|, quoted strings, special characters in IDs.

3. SEQUENCE PARSER: participants, actors, messages (solid/dashed/open), activations, notes (left/right/over), loops, alt/opt/par fragments, autonumber.

4. CLASS PARSER: class declarations, members (attributes+methods), relationships (inheritance <|-, composition *-, aggregation o-, etc.), cardinality, annotations/stereotypes.

5. STATE PARSER: states, transitions, start/end markers, nested states, fork/join, choice, notes.

6. GANTT PARSER: title, dateFormat, sections, tasks with dates/durations, dependencies (after), milestones, exclusions.

7. PIE PARSER: title, showData, slices with labels and values.

8. ERROR RECOVERY: malformed input never panics, dangling edges create placeholder nodes, fuzzy keyword matching works, mixed syntax detection, diagnostic messages are attached to IR.

9. DOT PARSER: digraph, graph, strict, attributes, subgraph, HTML labels, escape sequences.

Logging: each test case logs input string, detected type, parsed IR summary (node count, edge count), and any diagnostics. Use tracing::info! for all test logging.

Target: 200+ tests in fm-parser. Every diagram type must have positive AND negative test cases.

## Acceptance Criteria

- All functionality explicitly described above is implemented fully; no scope reduction, no feature deletions, and no silent behavioral regressions.
- Code is modular, documented where needed, and integrated with existing crates/interfaces without compatibility shims or duplicated logic.
- Comprehensive automated tests are added/updated for this issue: focused unit tests + relevant integration coverage for touched behavior and edge cases.
- End-to-end validation is covered where applicable (CLI and/or WASM path) with detailed structured logging (inputs, detection results, node/edge counts, output sizes, timings, and pass/fail reasons).
- Diagnostics are actionable: failures/warnings include precise context and suggested remediation.
- Determinism and stability expectations are verified for affected outputs (layout/render/serialization as applicable).
- Quality gates pass: `cargo fmt --check`, `cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings`, `cargo check --all-targets`, and relevant `cargo test` scope.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-11T18:18:13.952684408Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-18T07:10:40.695685322Z","closed_at":"2026-03-18T07:10:40.694949098Z","close_reason":"Added 48 new tests to fm-parser (164 -> 212 total). Coverage: detection per diagram type (13 tests), gantt (3), pie (3), C4 variants (5), error recovery (7), flowchart arrows/shapes (5), xychart (2), architecture (1), quadrant (1), requirement (2), block-beta (1), packet (1), DOT (2), determinism (1). All 212 tests pass, clippy clean.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["parser","testing","unit-tests"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2xl.7","depends_on_id":"bd-106","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T08:37:48Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2xl.7","depends_on_id":"bd-2xl","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-12T08:37:48Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2xl.7","depends_on_id":"bd-3bc.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T08:37:48Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-2xl.8","title":"Write comprehensive unit tests for fm-layout algorithms","description":"Write exhaustive unit tests for all layout algorithms. Test categories:

1. SUGIYAMA PIPELINE (per phase):
   a. Cycle removal: DAG (no cycles, no reversals), single cycle (A->B->C->A), nested cycles, self-loop, large SCC. Verify: reversed edge count, edge list matches, deterministic output.
   b. Rank assignment: linear chain (ranks 0,1,2...), diamond shape (A->B,A->C,B->D,C->D), wide graph. Verify: rank ordering respects edges, no rank gaps.
   c. Crossing minimization: known-optimal layout (verify 0 crossings), deliberately crossed layout (verify reduction). Verify: crossing count strictly non-increasing per iteration.
   d. Coordinate assignment: verify spacing constraints, label width expansion, no overlap. Verify: DiagramLayout.bounds encloses all nodes.

2. DETERMINISM: run same graph 100 times, verify byte-identical DiagramLayout output every time. Use proptest with fixed seeds.

3. FORCE-DIRECTED: small graph (5 nodes), medium (50 nodes), disconnected components. Verify: no node overlap, connected nodes closer than disconnected, deterministic from same input.

4. TREE LAYOUT: binary tree, unbalanced tree, forest (multiple roots). Verify: parent above children, no sibling overlap, correct orientation (TB/LR/RL/BT).

5. EDGE CASES: empty graph (0 nodes), single node, two disconnected nodes, fully connected K5, star graph, long chain (100 nodes).

6. QUALITY METRICS: verify LayoutStats fields are populated and sensible (crossings >= 0, iterations > 0, etc.).

Logging: each test logs graph description, algorithm used, iteration count, final crossing count, total edge length, and elapsed time. Use tracing::info! with structured fields.

Target: 100+ tests in fm-layout.

## Acceptance Criteria

- All functionality explicitly described above is implemented fully; no scope reduction, no feature deletions, and no silent behavioral regressions.
- Code is modular, documented where needed, and integrated with existing crates/interfaces without compatibility shims or duplicated logic.
- Comprehensive automated tests are added/updated for this issue: focused unit tests + relevant integration coverage for touched behavior and edge cases.
- End-to-end validation is covered where applicable (CLI and/or WASM path) with detailed structured logging (inputs, detection results, node/edge counts, output sizes, timings, and pass/fail reasons).
- Diagnostics are actionable: failures/warnings include precise context and suggested remediation.
- Determinism and stability expectations are verified for affected outputs (layout/render/serialization as applicable).
- Quality gates pass: `cargo fmt --check`, `cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings`, `cargo check --all-targets`, and relevant `cargo test` scope.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-11T18:18:31.069223858Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-27T20:24:20.957415853Z","closed_at":"2026-03-27T20:24:20.957133278Z","close_reason":"197 unit tests in fm-layout covering all layout algorithms (Sugiyama, force-directed, tree, radial, sequence, timeline, gantt, sankey, kanban, grid), cycle strategies (greedy/DFS/MFAS), crossing minimization, Brandes-Köpf, edge routing, guardrails, guard reports, decision ledger, dispatch, block-beta, and 5 proptest properties.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["layout","testing","unit-tests"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2xl.8","depends_on_id":"bd-2xl","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-12T08:37:48Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2xl.8","depends_on_id":"bd-3bc.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T08:37:48Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2xl.8","depends_on_id":"bd-vb9","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T08:37:48Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
